_note: How ingredients move between states. prep_classes gate which transitions exist (structural, not name-string — plan §0.4). coarse offsets adjust cut keep-life by coarseness. res_map binds transition→station; cook_browns binds method→browning. group_exceptions (live: read by prepGroups) lists items that must break a like-cut batch, with reasons. v2: edge_kinetics binds heat-bearing transition edges to the kinetics primitive, and cook_doneness gives kinetics the doneness/safety floor it uses to derive or validate cook duration.

edge_kinetics._note: Binds heat-bearing transition edges to the kinetics primitive (kinetics edges on state transitions). Presence here is load-bearing: the engine only annotates an edge whose verb appears below. cook → env/tau/T_end/brownedness/brownLabel(+egg doneness); temper/cool → target threshold the edge drives toward.

cook_doneness._note: Doneness/safety floor kinetics uses to derive or validate cook duration. by_group sets a safe internal temp per catalog group; egg uses the two-zone yolk model. Cook duration = max(recipe.cookMin, time-to-target); egg duration is target-derived. A per-recipe cookTo:{core_C|soft_C|yolk} overrides these. Seeded food-safety estimates — verify:true, cite a regional authority before trusting; do not fabricate thresholds.

group_exceptions.why: crushed via grind, not batched with the dicing pass
raw meat on a separate board; never grouped with veg cuts
raw poultry on a separate board + sanitise after
raw sausage on the meat board
peel choice flips keep-life (skin-on keeps longer) — group only within the chosen route
